subject: Biology
title: Active Transport
description: A quick, practical guide to Active Transport.

hook.text: Sometimes cells need to push molecules the wrong way—from low concentration to high.

buildup.text: Active transport uses ATP energy to pump molecules against their natural gradient.

discovery.text: The sodium-potassium pump moves 3 sodium ions out and 2 potassium ions in per ATP.

twist.text: Your brain spends about 70% of its energy just running these pumps in neurons.

climax.text: Without active transport, nerve signals, muscle contractions, and heartbeats all stop.

quiz.question: What does the sodium-potassium pump require to function?
